entities.name: WATER DEFICIT
entities.description: A prominent abiotic stress, also known as drought, that severely limits crop yields and livelihoods in semi-arid tropics

entities.name: CROP YIELDS
entities.description: The measure of agricultural productivity which is severely limited by water deficit

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Geographic regions characterized by low and erratic rainfall patterns where water deficit is a major stress

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: An agricultural crop that suffers significant annual economic losses due to drought

entities.name: MODERN SCIENCE TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Advanced scientific approaches used to develop crops that are more resilient to low and erratic rainfall

entities.name: LOW AND ERRATIC RAINFALL PATTERNS
entities.description: Environmental conditions that necessitate the development of resilient crop varieties

entities.name: CONVENTIONAL BREEDING
entities.description: A traditional breeding approach used to develop early maturing varieties to escape drought

entities.name: EARLY MATURITY
entities.description: The ability of a crop variety to mature quickly to escape severe water deficit

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to withstand water deficit, which can be achieved through earliness or other mechanisms

entities.name: YIELD-BASED SELECTION
entities.description: A conventional selection method used to identify and select for drought tolerance
